note: The correct half of the strip-suffix pair, same ticket, using the stdlib method PEP 616 added for exactly this problem. Its job is to catch a judge that blocks everything: a sensor that says not_satisfied to both halves of a pair has not read either. Every claim below was verified by running it.

labels.text: only a trailing ".com" is removed; characters elsewhere in the hostname are left alone
labels.why: removesuffix matches the whole substring at the end and removes it only there. Verified: 'microsoft.com' -> 'microsoft', 'comcast.com' -> 'comcast', 'mom.com' -> 'mom'.

labels.text: a hostname that does not end in ".com" is returned unchanged
labels.why: removesuffix returns the string untouched when the suffix is absent. Verified: 'example.org' -> 'example.org'.
